## Page 453

### [p453_c0001] AGREED-UPON PROCEDURES PERFORMED IN CONNECTION WITH THE MTA IG'S BUDGET ACCOUNTABILITY FOR THE YEAR ENDED DECEMBER 31, 2024
[p453_c0002] July 28, 2025
### [p453_c0003] EXHIBIT A
[p453_c0004] The procedures and the associated findings are as follows:
- [p453_c0005] 1. We utilized a random number generator to randomly select five employees from the MTA Headquarters - IG payroll register for the month of December 2024 and we compared each individual's salary to an approved Personnel Action Form obtained from the IG management ("management").
[p453_c0006] We found no exceptions as a result of the procedures.
- [p453_c0007] 2. We utilized a random number generator to randomly select two terminated employees from the listing of terminated employees for the year ended December 31, 2024 and obtained each individual's Personnel Action Form for completeness. We inspected the payroll register subsequent to each employee's termination and inspected these employees were removed from the payroll register.
[p453_c0008] We found no exceptions as a result of the procedures.
- [p453_c0009] 3. We compared the IG's total payroll per the payroll register for the month of December 2024 to the MTA's monthly expense report for the month of December 2024 and observed they agreed.
[p453_c0010] We found no exceptions as a result of the procedures.
- [p453_c0011] 4. We utilized a random number generator to randomly select five purchases made during the year from the IG's general ledger for the year ended December 31, 2024. For each purchase, we obtained from management the related requisitions and purchase orders and observed an approver's sign off.
[p453_c0012] We found no exceptions as a result of the procedures.
- [p453_c0013] 5. We utilized a random number generator to randomly select fifteen disbursements from the IG's General Fund for the year ended December 31, 2024 and compared the selected disbursements to supporting documentation including invoices, purchases orders and cancelled checks obtained from management. For each selected disbursement, we observed the reimbursement recorded agreed to the invoice, purchase order, and cancelled check and was also signed off by management as evidence of authorization.
[p453_c0014] We found no exceptions as a result of the procedures.
- [p453_c0015] 6. We utilized a random number generator to randomly select five petty cash vouchers from the IG's general ledger for the three months ended December 31, 2024, and obtained the respective voucher from management. For each selection, we observed the voucher was approved and signed off by management in accordance with IG Petty Cash Policy.
[p453_c0016] _2_
[p453_c0017] _Master Page # 453 of 454 - MTA Board Meeting 7/30/2025_

## Page 454

### [p454_c0001] AGREED-UPON PROCEDURES PERFORMED IN CONNECTION WITH THE MTA IG'S BUDGET ACCOUNTABILITY FOR THE YEAR ENDED DECEMBER 31, 2024
[p454_c0002] We found no exceptions as a result of the procedures.
- [p454_c0003] 7. We compared the cash balance per the Cash Inspector General Fund's bank reconciliation for the month of December 2024 to the Authority's general ledger and observed they both agreed.
[p454_c0004] We found no exceptions as result of the procedures.
- [p454_c0005] 8. We utilized a random number generator to randomly select five employee reimbursements from the IG listing of employees' reimbursements for the year ended December 31, 2024. We observed the reimbursements were supported by invoices, purchase orders, bank statements, reimbursement applications and refund requisitions provided by management. We also utilized a random number generator to randomly select five employee expense reports from the IG listing of employee expenses reports for the year ended December 31, 2024 and observed the expense reports were supported by invoices, purchase orders, and proof of payment such as bank statements.
[p454_c0006] We found no exceptions as a result of the procedures.
- [p454_c0007] 9. We obtained a listing of fixed asset additions for the year ended December 31, 2024 from management. We observed that one fixed asset addition was made during the year ended December 31, 2024. For the fixed asset addition, we obtained the respective invoice, purchase order, and payment support from management. We observed management sign off of approval on the purchase orders and agreed the amounts in the invoices to proof of payment such as bank statements.
[p454_c0008] We found no exceptions as a result of the procedures.
- [p454_c0009] 10. We inquired of management whether there were any fixed asset disposals made during the year ended December 31, 2024 for the IG, and management indicated there were none. 11. We recalculated the depreciation expense amount for all fixed assets with depreciation expense recorded in the IG's general ledger for the month of December 2024 that were in excess of $1,000.
[p454_c0010] We found no exceptions as a result of the procedures.
- [p454_c0011] 12. We compared the IG's general ledger depreciation expense for the month of December 2024 to the MTA's general ledger and found them to be in agreement.
[p454_c0012] We found no exceptions as a result of the procedures.
- [p454_c0013] 13. We compared the IG's total monthly expense report to the MTA's total monthly expense report for the month of December 2024. We observed that there was a difference of $18,881. We were informed the difference is a direct result of professional service contracts incurred during the year that are required for the MTA's year-end accrual process.
- [p454_c0014] 14. We obtained from management the IG's 2024 total expense budget and the actual total expenditures for the year ended December 31, 2024. We compared these reports and observed that total actual expenditures were less than the IG's 2024 total expense budget by $3,868,182.
